mechanism_narrative: Sustained monthly payroll growth (PAYEMS) above 150k persons signals robust labor demand and consumer income expansion, which drives equity valuations higher through increased corporate earnings expectations and consumer spending. This always-on trend mechanism predicts positive S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days when payroll momentum is positive.
evidence_refs: fact:May 2012 PAYEMS at 135.8M persons indicates ongoing labor market recovery post-2008
regime_hint: post-shock recovery with labor market normalization
failure_condition: Payroll growth remains positive but S&P 500 produces negative 20-day returns in more than 55% of months where PAYEMS month-over-month change exceeds 100k persons, or Rank IC < 0.05 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS) exceeds 5.0% and remains elevated for 2+ months, the Fed's restrictive monetary stance increases corporate borrowing costs and reduces equity risk appetite, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns. This threshold-triggered mechanism activates only when policy rates cross into restrictive territory.
evidence_refs: fact:May 2012 FEDFUNDS at 5.25% indicates elevated policy rates
regime_hint: monetary tightening cycle with restrictive rates
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 20-day returns in more than 60% of instances where FEDFUNDS > 5.0% for 2+ months, or the correlation between FEDFUNDS level and subsequent 20-day returns is positive (r > 0.10) over any 2-year window.

mechanism_narrative: A joint contraction in retail sales (RSAFS) and housing starts (HOUST) signals weakening aggregate demand and consumer confidence, which depresses corporate profit expectations and equity valuations. This interaction-driven mechanism requires both demand proxies to decline simultaneously to activate the negative return signal.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS and HOUST both decline month-over-month for 2 consecutive months
evidence_refs: fact:May 2012 HOUST at 1.772M units and RSAFS at 363.7B dollars show mixed demand signals
regime_hint: late-cycle slowdown or demand-driven recession risk
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 60-day returns in more than 65% of instances where both RSAFS and HOUST decline for 2 consecutive months, or Rank IC of the interaction term < 0.04 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) growth above 1.5% year-over-year signals robust supply-side capacity utilization and manufacturing momentum, which supports corporate profitability and equity returns. This always-on trend mechanism predicts positive S&P 500 returns over 5 trading days when industrial production momentum is strong.
evidence_refs: fact:May 2012 INDPRO at 113.13 (2017=100) reflects post-crisis manufacturing recovery
regime_hint: any regime with positive supply-side momentum
failure_condition: INDPRO year-over-year growth exceeds 1.5% but S&P 500 produces negative 5-day returns in more than 50% of such instances, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: An unemployment rate (UNRATE) increase of more than 0.3 percentage points within a 3-month window signals deteriorating labor market conditions and reduced consumer purchasing power, triggering negative equity returns as investors reprrice earnings expectations downward. This threshold-triggered mechanism activates only when unemployment shocks exceed the tolerance threshold.
evidence_refs: fact:May 2012 UNRATE at 4.4% represents improved labor market from 2009-2011 lows
regime_hint: labor market deterioration or recession onset
failure_condition: UNRATE increases > 0.3pp within 3 months but S&P 500 produces positive 20-day returns in more than 55% of such instances, or the correlation between UNRATE changes and subsequent 20-day returns is positive (r > 0.08) over any 2-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Declining inflation (CPIAUCSL month-over-month growth < 0.2%) combined with elevated federal funds rates (FEDFUNDS > 4.5%) creates a regime of real monetary tightening that compresses equity valuations and reduces real asset returns. This interaction-driven mechanism requires both monetary conditions to align to activate the negative signal.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL MoM growth < 0.2% AND FEDFUNDS > 4.5% sustained for 2+ months
evidence_refs: fact:May 2012 CPIAUCSL at 202.7 and FEDFUNDS at 5.25% indicate potential real rate tightening
failure_condition: Real monetary tightening conditions persist but S&P 500 produces positive 60-day returns in more than 60% of instances, or the interaction term Rank IC < 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth (GDPC1) acceleration above 2.5% annualized signals robust economic expansion and rising corporate earnings, producing positive S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days. This regime-conditional mechanism is most powerful in late-cycle expansions when growth surprises to the upside relative to trend expectations.
evidence_refs: fact:Q1 2012 GDPC1 at 11432.9B (2017 dollars) reflects ongoing post-crisis recovery
failure_condition: GDPC1 annualized growth exceeds 2.5% but S&P 500 produces negative 60-day returns in more than 50% of such quarters, or Rank IC < 0.04 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts (HOUST) declining below 1.5M annualized units signals weakening residential investment and forward-looking demand weakness, which predicts negative S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days as investors anticipate reduced construction employment and consumer wealth effects from housing.
evidence_refs: fact:May 2012 HOUST at 1.772M units above threshold but vulnerable to decline
regime_hint: housing market weakness or demand-driven slowdown
failure_condition: HOUST falls below 1.5M annualized units but S&P 500 produces positive 20-day returns in more than 55% of such instances, or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window.
